Rows: CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.PT >= 14 AND T1.SEX = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)

pred_sql:
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.PT >= 14 AND T1.SEX = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient AS T1 
INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID 
WHERE (strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', T1.Birthday)) > 55 
AND T2.PT IS NOT NULL;
question: For all patients who are older than 55 years old, what is the percentage of female who has abnormal prothrombin time (PT)?
